TIMOTHY P, HAYES
Mr. Scott Hullabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practice Commission
428 | Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95874
Re:
Request for Written Advice Re Whether Conflict of
Interests Exist Regarding Annexations to City of Lincoln
Dear Mr. Hullabrin:
The City of Lincoln will soon be acting upon several pending annexation
requests comprising approximately 3,000 acres, or 4.7 square miles. The property is
currently in Placer County and zoned agriculture. If annexed into the City, it is
anticipated that a variety of zoning uses will be implemented, consisting mainly of
single-family housing, but also including open space, industrial and commercial.
The pending annexations will affect nearly 2.5 miles of the southerly and
southeasterly boundaries of the City and it is in that portion of the City, i.e., the
southeasterly quadrant of the City, where nearly all of the population of Lincoln
currently resides. Enclosed is a copy of the map of the City showing the developed
areas, the City's boundaries, and the "Pending Applications" and "Potential Areas of
Application" for annexation.
In view of Lincoln's small size, i.e., approximately 6.8 square miles, and
population of approximately 7,500 residents, the magnitude and location of the
potential annexations obviously leads to a potential for a conflict of interest, and the
City wishes to receive formal written advice from the Fair Political Practice
Commission as to any potential conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act
("Ac{').
lhe C_ity is seeking formal written advice as to potential conflicts of interests
of the City Council members (excluding Ron Barringer who, because of his business
interests, i.e., real estate broker, we -huve alread! informed has a conflict) the
Planning Commission members, City Administraior, Richard Ramirez, and the
Director of Community Development, Rodney Campbell.


Mr. Scott Hullabrin
August 9, 1991
Page2
All of the principal residences of the City Council members and the Planning
Commission members are located in the City of Lincoln, and all their residences are
more than 300 feet from any pending or potential annexation applications. As a
result, it would appear that, as to the principal residences of the City Council and
Planning Commissioners, pursuant to Code of Regulation 1,8703.1,, there is not any
potential conflict under the Act as any effect of the annexation on the principal
residences would not be distinguishable from the effect on the public generally.
Councilman Carl Malotte, in addition to his principal residence, also owns
greater than a 10 per cent share in Malotte Manufacturing, Inc., which manufactures
fiberglass. He also has a real property interest in the property his company leases.
The company and property are located at 390 "C" Street, which is approximately
2,1,25 feet from a pending annexation, approximately 3,000 feet from a potential
annexation, and approxim alely 2,750 feet from another pending annexation.
Councilman Stanley P. Nader, in addition to his principal residence, also
owns greater than a 10 per cent interest in a family farming operation which consists
of real property and a feed store which are located outside the City limits, north of
the northwest corner of the City, west of Highway 65 and north of Wise Road, which
is over three miles from the closest pending or proposed annexation.
Councilman Charles A. Kellar's principal residence is located on two lots at
420-7th Street, approximately 2,725 feet from a potential annexation and 2500 feet
from a pending annexation. Also on the property is a separate guest house which
Mr. Kellar has never rented but has allowed his relatives to live in it.
_ Planning Commissioners Marshall weir and Eva Benedict own real property
within the City in addition to their principal residences. Mr. weir's real property
interests consist of (i) a deed of trust in a residence at 565 East Avenue'and (ii)
ownership of a vacant,parcel immediately to the south of his property at 565 East
Avenue, both of which are approximately 430 feet from a fot""tiri annexation
requ€st and 7,875 feet from a pending annexation and 5,00b feet from another
pending annexation, (iii) oravn"r'rhto oJ a vacant parcel immediately to the east of his
property at 565 East Avenue, and said vacant pa;cel lies immediately to the west of a
f:tentlal. annexation request and is within 300 feet of said potJ"iiuiu""exation
request, 1,875 feet from a pending annexation and 5,000 feet ffom ""o*,". p""ai"g
annexation and (iv) ownership 
- of .vacant property at 121 Ina way r.,ihich is
approximately 350 feet flgT_ i pending ar,.reruiion, 2,250 feet from "a potential
annexation request and 2,490 feet from a"nother penainjanne."i.., *air5 a deed


Mr. Scott Hullabrin
August 9, 1991
Page 3
of trust on a residence at 330 E Street which is approximately 7375 feet from a
pending annexation, 2250 feet from a potential annexations and 2750 feet from
another pending annexation.
Eva Benedict owns (i) real properties at 510 and 512 D Street consisting of
rentals which are approxim ately 7,750 feet from the nearest pending annexation
request/ 4,000 feet from another pending annexation and 5,000 feet frorn another
pending annexation, (ii) real property at 670 M Street consisting of rentals which is
approximately 3,750 feet from the nearest pending annexation, and (iii) rental real
property at 143 E Fifth Street which is approximately 270 feet from the closest
potential annexation request and 500 feet from an actual pending annexation.
The principal residence of Richard Ramirez, the City Administrator, is located
within 300 feet of an actual pending annexation and 2,750 feet from another pending
annexation. Rod Campbell, the Director of Community Development, owns a real
property interest in a home his parents live in (no rent is charged) which is located
approximately 1,750 feet from the nearest potential annexation request and
approimately 2,000 feet from the nearest actual pending annexation.
As indicated above, the current square mile area of the City is approximately
6.8 square miles. As shown in the enclosed map, nearly all of the existing
development of the City has occurred in the southeasterly portion of the City in an
area slightly greater than two square miles the borders of which are essentially
encompassed by the nearly 2.5 mileJong pending annexation areas. It is estimated
that approxim ately 50vo of the developed residential area of the city lies within 2,500
feet of the pending annexation property and nearly 100% lies within 5,000 feet of the
pending annexation property. As a result, because of the relatively small size of the
City compared with the magnitude of the pending annexation requests and since the
pending annexations encompasses that portion of the City whicir has already been
developed, it would seem that a significint segment of the citizens of Lincoln, if not
everyone, 
. will be impacted in the same manner as will the public officials.
Accordingly, in addition to .the Act considering the principal residences which lie
beyond 300 feet as not resulting in a conflict sinie the-residences would be impacted
substantially in the same^manner.as wourd the public generaily, iil p"ir"u"t ,o
Code.of Regulations 18703 that the City,s officials, otier real'prop".iv 
-inte.est,
would not constitute a conflict of inteiest because tt" "ri"ci 5"-Ir,"'i"iiriarur
officials'other interests by the annexations w r be substantialy ttre same as *re
effect upon a significant segment of the citizens of Lincoln.


Mr. Scott Hullabrin
August 9, 1991
Page4
I realize that this is a somewhat complicated request as it seeks advice as to
several different conflict of interest issues, and if you need further information,
please do not hesitate to contact me. The City of Lincoln thanks you for your
assistance in this matter.
Very truly yours,
*-a,p/L*
TIMOTHY P. I{AYES

Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practice Commission
4281Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re:
Request for written advice re whether conflict of interest
exists regarding annexations to the City of Lincoln
Dear Mr. Hullabrin:
It has been brought to my attention that with respect to my August g,7gg1,
letter to you concerning the above-referenced matter, it is necessary to make a
correction to the second full paragraph on page 2 that describes the financial
interests of Councilman Carl Malotte. Mr. Malotte does not lease the property and,
rather, has a month-to-month tenancy. As a result, it is my understanding,
pursuant to the Political Reform Act, that would not constitute a property interest.
Hence, the second sentence of the above-referenced paragraph should be deleted.
Please contact me if you have any questions on this matter.
TPH:jkt
cc: Carl Malotte
City Council Member
Richard Ramirez
City Administrator
b
Very truly yours,
TIMOTHY P. HAYEI

sections.question: Whether conflict of interests exist regarding annexations to the City of Lincoln, specifically whether City Council members (excluding Ron Barringer), Planning Commission members, City Administrator Richard Ramirez, and Director of Community Development Rodney Campbell have potential conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act based on their real property interests and the pending/potential annexation applications.
sections.question_synthetic: The City of Lincoln seeks formal written advice from the FPPC regarding potential conflicts of interest for City officials in connection with pending annexations of approximately 3,000 acres that could significantly impact the city's composition and values of nearby properties. The inquiry covers multiple city officials' real property interests and their proximity to proposed annexation areas.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No question or conclusion sections are explicitly labeled. The letter presents the legal question within the body text and includes detailed factual background. A supplemental letter dated August 14, 1991 (appears to be a typo for 1992 based on year 1997 shown) was included as a correction regarding Councilman Malotte's property interest description.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from the law firm representing the City of Lincoln to the FPPC seeking formal written advice on potential conflicts of interest for city officials regarding pending and potential annexations of approximately 3,000 acres. The letter includes detailed descriptions of city officials' real property interests and their distances from the annexation applications, with a supplemental correction letter noting an error regarding Councilman Malotte's property interest.
